context: Tests whether the agent follows rails-graphql-best-practices: using dataloaders for association loads, applying field-level auth on sensitive fields, returning structured { result, errors } from mutations, disabling introspection in production, setting query depth/complexity limits, using dedicated resolver classes, connection types for paginated fields, and schema.execute in tests.

checklist.name: Dataloader for association
checklist.description: ListResolver loads the buyer association through a dataloader (dataloader.with(...).load(...)) rather than calling object.buyer or object.buyer_id directly

checklist.name: Field-level guard on sensitive field
checklist.description: The internal_notes field in Types::OrderType has its own field-level authorization guard (not just relying on type-level auth)

checklist.name: No type-only auth for sensitive field
checklist.description: Types::OrderType does NOT rely solely on a class-level guard — internal_notes has a separate guard block or before_check

checklist.name: Mutation returns errors array
checklist.description: Mutations::PlaceOrder defines a field :errors of array type (e.g. [String]) that is always present in the response

checklist.name: Mutation no unhandled raise
checklist.description: Mutations::PlaceOrder does NOT raise unhandled exceptions to the caller — errors are caught and returned in the errors field

checklist.name: Introspection disabled in production
checklist.description: AppSchema calls disable_introspection_entry_points (conditionally for production environment, or unconditionally)

checklist.name: max_depth set
checklist.description: AppSchema sets max_depth with a numeric value

checklist.name: max_complexity set
checklist.description: AppSchema sets max_complexity with a numeric value

checklist.name: Dedicated resolver class
checklist.description: Orders list resolution is in a dedicated Resolvers::Orders::ListResolver class, not as an inline field block in QueryType

checklist.name: Connection type for paginated list
checklist.description: The orders field (wherever it is defined) uses .connection_type (e.g. Types::OrderType.connection_type) rather than a plain array type

checklist.name: schema.execute in specs
checklist.description: Both spec files use AppSchema.execute(...) to run queries/mutations — not controller dispatch or request specs via HTTP

checklist.name: descriptions on fields
checklist.description: Types::OrderType includes description calls on at least 3 of its fields
